words.name: Snake
words.description:
A long, legless reptile that moves by gliding on its belly.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'bent V' handshape (middle and index bent).
2. Move the hand forward in a serpentine (wiggling) motion.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'bent V' handshape (middle and index bent). 2. Move the hand forward in a serpentine (wiggling) motion.

words.name: Lizard
words.description:
A reptile typically having a long body, four legs, and a tapering tail.
How to sign:
1. Use the dominant hand in the 'L' handshape.
2. Move the hand in a crawling motion up the non-dominant forearm.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the dominant hand in the 'L' handshape. 2. Move the hand in a crawling motion up the non-dominant forearm.

words.name: Turtle
words.description:
A slow-moving reptile encased in a shell.
How to sign:
1. Use the non-dominant hand as a 'dome' (shell).
2. Use the dominant 'A' hand (head/neck).
3. Tuck the 'A' hand under the dome, then move it forward and out.
words.howToSign: 1. Use the non-dominant hand as a 'dome' (shell). 2. Use the dominant 'A' hand (head/neck). 3. Tuck the 'A' hand under the dome, then move it forward and out.

words.name: Crocodile
words.description:
A large predatory semiaquatic reptile with a long jaw and powerful tail.
How to sign:
1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape, held flat and stacked at the wrist.
2. Open and close the hands like a large jaw.
words.howToSign: 1. Use both hands in the '5' handshape, held flat and stacked at the wrist. 2. Open and close the hands like a large jaw.

words.name: Alligator
words.description:
A large reptile with a shorter, blunter snout than a crocodile.
How to sign:
1. Same sign as Crocodile.
